quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Today is the summer solstice, the longest day of the year in the Northern Hemisphere! In Hungary, this day is celebrated with ancient traditions including lighting bonfires and gathering medicinal herbs, which were believed to have special powers when picked on this magical day.

short_read.title: The Magic of Summer Solstice Around the World
short_read.teaser: From Sweden's Midsummer celebrations to Hungary's ancient herb-gathering traditions, cultures worldwide honor the longest day of the year. Discover how people have celebrated this astronomical event for thousands of years.

medium_read.title: The Secret Lives of Harbor Seals in the Strait of Juan de Fuca
medium_read.teaser: These charming marine mammals are year-round residents of the waters you see from Port Angeles. Learn about their playful personalities, impressive diving abilities, and the important role they play in the local ecosystem.

long_read.title: The Lavender Fields of the Olympic Peninsula
long_read.teaser: How the Sequim-Dungeness Valley became the "Lavender Capital of North America" is a story of climate, passion, and community. This beautiful corner of the Peninsula shares a surprising connection with the lavender fields of Provence, France.

port_angeles.title: Black-Tailed Deer at Hurricane Ridge
port_angeles.description: June is a wonderful time to spot black-tailed deer and their adorable fawns at Hurricane Ridge. These gentle creatures are often seen grazing in the meadows, especially in the early morning and evening hours. The drive up offers stunning views of the Strait and wildflowers in full bloom.

puzzle_1.question: On the summer solstice, how many hours of daylight does Port Angeles receive? (approximately ___ hours)
puzzle_1.correct_response: That's right, Barbara! Port Angeles enjoys about 16 glorious hours of daylight today — perfect for enjoying the beauty around you!
puzzle_1.close_response: Very close! Port Angeles actually gets about 16 hours of daylight on the summer solstice — what a long, beautiful day!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: Port Angeles receives about 16 hours of daylight on the summer solstice — the longest day of the year! That's a lot of time to enjoy the sunshine.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: Because Port Angeles is at 48 degrees north latitude, it experiences dramatic seasonal variations in daylight. In contrast, on the winter solstice in December, there are only about 8 hours of daylight!

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Beautifully done! Pillangó is indeed the Hungarian word for butterfly — such a lovely, musical word!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The Hungarian word for butterfly is 'pillangó' — doesn't it sound just as delicate and graceful as the creature itself?
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian has many beautiful nature words. Virág means flower, madár means bird, and méhecske means little bee. The language is known for its poetic and descriptive vocabulary.

puzzle_3.question: What type of whale is most commonly seen from Port Angeles in the summer months?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Exactly right! Orcas are frequently spotted in the Strait of Juan de Fuca during summer, especially the Southern Resident pods who come here to feed on salmon. What magnificent creatures to have as neighbors!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: The most common whales to spot from Port Angeles in summer are actually Orcas! The Southern Resident killer whales visit the Strait of Juan de Fuca to feast on salmon during these months.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Three pods of Southern Resident orcas — J, K, and L pods — spend their summers in the waters around the San Juan Islands and the Strait of Juan de Fuca. These family groups are made up of about 75 whales total, and they're a beloved part of the local marine ecosystem.
puzzle_3.learn_more_label: Learn About Local Orcas — Whale Museum

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
